summary: Conjecture 4.3 from arXiv:2404.02021 asks for the existence of a 3-graph H achieving the purely exponential rate r(H, K_n^(3)) = 2^{\Theta_H(n)}, in contrast to the 2^{\Theta_H(n \log n)} established for many H in the source paper. No paper confirming or refuting this conjecture was found. A November 2024 follow-up (arXiv:2411.13812) characterises polynomial growth for tightly-connected 3-graphs, and a July 2025 paper (arXiv:2507.09434) studies the polynomial-to-exponential transition for 3-uniform Ramsey numbers via an Erdos-Hajnal-type result, but neither was confirmed to directly resolve whether the 2^{\Theta(n)} rate is achievable.

since_posted.title: When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?
since_posted.claim: Characterises, for tightly-connected 3-graphs H (or those with at most two tight components), exactly when r(H, K_n^(3)) grows polynomially, addressing the boundary of the exponential regime but not the 2^{Theta(n)} conjecture directly.

since_posted.title: Polynomial-to-exponential transition in 3-uniform Ramsey numbers
since_posted.claim: Proves an Erdos-Hajnal-type result establishing a polynomial-to-exponential threshold for 3-uniform Ramsey numbers; relation to the 2^{Theta(n)} conjecture of arXiv:2404.02021 could not be confirmed from the abstract alone.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about induced subgraph density in graphs of bounded VC-dimension (Fox-Pach-Suk conjecture on polynomial clique/stable-set size); it has no connection to hypergraph Ramsey numbers or r(H, K_n^(3)). The internal-reference match is a false positive.

search_queries: off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers 3-graph exponential rate Conlon Fox Mubayi Suk 2404.02021
r(H, K_n^3) 3-uniform hypergraph Ramsey exponential 2^{Theta(n)} conjecture
notes: The conjecture is distinct from the diagonal Ramsey tower problem; the source paper already achieves 2^{Omega(n)} lower bounds for a candidate H with 1/3 < m_pair(H) < 1/2, making the gap to 2^{Theta(n)} an upper-bound question. Active follow-up work (2411.13812, 2507.09434) is ongoing in this area but no resolution of the conjecture was found within the 5-call budget.
